articles.title: Ro Khanna detained by Israeli settlers during occupied West Bank visit
articles.summary: Ro Khanna detained by Israeli settlers during occupied West Bank visit US Democratic lawmaker Ro Khanna said he was detained by Israeli settlers armed with US-made rifles during a visit this week to the occupied West Bank. Speaking with Reuters from a Palestinian village, Khanna said his group's van was surrounded by settlers wielding M4 rifles on Wednesday while touring a part of the southern West Bank where residents face frequent Israeli settler attacks. articles.title: Israeli troops push into Syrian village and briefly establish checkpoint
articles.summary: Israeli troops push into Syrian village and briefly establish checkpoint Israeli forces briefly carried out an incursion into the Syrian village of al-Samdaniyah in Quneitra, Syrian state media has reported.  Sana news agency reported that three Israeli army vehicles pushed into al-Samdaniyah on Saturday morning and established a temporary checkpoint.  Israeli troops searched passersby at the checkpoint, before later withdrawing. Two days ago, Israeli troops carried out a similar action, when over 15 army vehicles entered al-Asha village in Quneitra’s southern countryside.

articles.title: Morning recap
articles.summary: Morning recap Good morning, Middle East Eye readers. Iran insisted on Saturday that it had "kept its word" on a ceasefire with the United States, after the US president, Donald Trump, insisted the truce was over but that he had agreed to further negotiations with the Islamic Republic. Iran’s foreign minister, Abbas Araghchi, said: "Violation follows other violations and missteps by the United States. articles.summary: Official: Iran suffered $300m in damage to research infrastructure Iran’s vice-president for science, technology and knowledge-based economy, Hossein Afshin, said knowledge-based companies also suffered heavy losses, Iranian media reported. Afshin said knowledge-based firms incurred losses of 65m rials and $25m in foreign exchange during the "imposed war", IRNA reported. "The enemy had targeted Iran's scientific and technological authority, which will be rebuilt with power," Afshin said.

articles.title: IEA: US-Iran escalation threatens 2027 oil surplus forecast

"An escalation in hostilities on 7-8 July, however, clouds the outlook and could upend the forecast that sees the market flipping to a surplus next year," it said, adding that a lasting peace agreement is a "must" for oil markets to normalise, according to Reuters.
